# Knowledge Graph Theory

## Graph Fundamentals

A graph $G = (V, E)$ consists of a set of vertices $V$ and edges $E \subseteq V \times V$. In a directed graph, edges have a source and a target; in an undirected graph, edges are symmetric.

## Centrality Metrics

### Degree Centrality

Degree centrality counts the number of edges incident to a node. In a knowledge graph, high-degree nodes are hubs connecting many concepts.

### Betweenness Centrality

Betweenness centrality measures how often a node lies on the shortest path between other node pairs, identifying bridge concepts.
